channel.title: CyberNetSec - Patch Management
channel.description: Latest patch management threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: cPanel Zero-Day Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-41940) Actively Exploited for Months Before Patch
item.description:
A critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-41940, in the widely used cPanel & WHM web hosting platform was actively exploited as a zero-day for at least two months before a patch was released. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to gain fu...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: MOVEit Automation Hit with Critical 9.8 CVSS Auth Bypass Flaw (CVE-2026-4670)
item.description:
Progress Software has issued an urgent security alert for a critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-4670, in its MOVEit Automation software. The flaw, which has been assigned a CVSS score of 9.8, could allow an unauthenticated attacker to gain unauthorized access and potentially admin...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical 'Copy Fail' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Gives Instant Root on Major Distros
item.description:
A critical logic flaw in the Linux kernel, dubbed 'Copy Fail' and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been disclosed, affecting nearly all major distributions since 2017. Discovered by Theori, the vulnerability allows any unprivileged local user to gain full root privileges with a simple, highly reliable...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Decade-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Full Root Access, Exploits Hard to Detect
item.description:
A critical remote code execution vulnerability, CVE-2026-35414, has been discovered in OpenSSH, affecting versions released over the last 15 years. The flaw, which carries a CVSS score of 8.1, resides in the parsing of SSH certificate principal names. An attacker with a valid certificate from a trus...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Foxit PDF Reader Flaw (CVE-2026-5942) Could Lead to Information Disclosure
item.description:
A use-after-free vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5942, has been disclosed in affected versions of Foxit PDF Reader. The flaw, which requires a user to open a malicious file, can be exploited to disclose sensitive information from the system's memory. While rated with a CVSS score of 3.3, security...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical Unauthenticated Path Traversal Flaw Found in CrowdStrike LogScale
item.description:
CrowdStrike has patched a critical, unauthenticated path traversal vulnerability, CVE-2026-40050, in its self-hosted LogScale (formerly Humio) log management platform. The vulnerability, discovered internally by CrowdStrike, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to read arbitrary files from...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Adobe Scrambles to Patch Critical, Actively Exploited RCE Flaw in Commerce and Magento
item.description:
Adobe has released an emergency, out-of-band security update for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, CVE-2026-11234, affecting Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, is being actively exploited in the wild. An unauthenticated attacker can e...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Log4j Deja Vu: Critical RCE Flaw in 'LogSpresso' Library Averts Major Supply Chain Crisis
item.description:
A potential Log4j-level crisis has been averted with the emergency patching of CVE-2026-23456, a critical (10.0 CVSS) remote code execution vulnerability in the popular open-source Java logging library 'LogSpresso'. Discovered by researchers at Checkmarx, the flaw was alarmingly similar to the infam...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Progress Patches Critical Command Injection Flaws in MOVEit WAF and LoadMaster
item.description:
Progress Software has released patches for a suite of vulnerabilities in its Application Delivery Controller (ADC) products, including MOVEit WAF and LoadMaster. The patched flaws include several authenticated command injection vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3517, CVE-2026-3519, CVE-2026-3518, CVE-2026-4...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Urgent Patching for Eight Actively Exploited Flaws in Cisco, JetBrains, and More
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has expanded its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog by adding eight new security flaws affecting a range of enterprise products. The vulnerabilities, found in software from Cisco, PaperCut, JetBrains, Kentico, Quest, and Syn...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →
